codeReview.summary: The scenarios exercise manual and pending-to-verified transitions thoroughly, but one cadence assertion is stricter than the feature contract and can fail contract-compliant behavior.

issues.description: The pending cadence assertion requires `len(window) >= 3` (and `len(gaps) >= 2`), which enforces at least three `/verify` polls inside 25s. The feature contract requires >=2 polls in 25s with 8-12s spacing, so a compliant two-poll implementation would fail this test.
`VERIFY_URL` is hardcoded to a specific Modal hostname and `verify_requests` matches full URL equality. Contract expectations are path-based (`/verify`), so this introduces avoidable host coupling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature verification instructions and skill procedure still include pytest `--timeout` flags even though this environment lacks `pytest-timeout`; workers must deviate to complete verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:897 includes `--timeout=90`; handoff.skillFeedback.deviations for this feature records `unrecognized arguments: --timeout=90` and rerunning without the flag.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared service shortcut `test-fast` still uses `--timeout=30`, which appears inconsistent with the current test environment and can cause avoidable validator/worker failures.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:4 defines `test-fast: cd client && uv run pytest -x --timeout=30`; the same timeout-plugin mismatch is documented in this feature handoff.

summary: Reviewed the transcript skeleton and commit diff for manual/pending scenarios. Coverage is strong overall, but the pending poll-window assertion currently over-constrains the contract (blocking), and verify-log matching is unnecessarily host-specific (non-blocking).
